issue_title: update forge to auto close issues when pr have been merged to main referencing the pr correctly
problem_statement: The forge pipeline does not currently auto-close GitHub issues when a PR referencing them is merged to main. GitHub supports auto-closing issues via keywords (e.g., 'Closes #N') in PR descriptions or commit messages. The pipeline's PR assembly templates and/or scripts need to be updated to ensure merged PRs correctly reference and auto-close their associated issues.
implementation_plan: Update `.antigravity/templates/pr-description.md` to include a GitHub closing keyword (e.g., `Closes #{{issue_number}}`) so that merged PRs automatically close the referenced issue.
Review and update `scripts/forge-create.sh` to ensure the issue number is passed through and interpolated into the PR description template when creating PRs.
Review `.antigravity/config.yml` for any configuration related to PR creation or issue linking and ensure the issue reference is wired through the pipeline.
Update `.agents/rules/pr-assembler.md` to instruct the PR assembler agent to always include a `Closes #<issue_id>` line in the PR body, ensuring correct issue-to-PR linkage.
acceptance_criteria: PR description template contains a GitHub closing keyword (e.g., `Closes #<issue_number>`) that is dynamically populated with the correct issue number.
When a PR created by the forge pipeline is merged to main, the referenced GitHub issue is automatically closed.
The issue number reference in the PR body is a valid GitHub issue link (not just a bare number).
Existing pipeline functionality (PR creation, commit messages) is not broken by the change.
